statement: Linear memory execution (minimal MVP subset). Extends the M1 integer core + M2 structured control flow with the Memory section, i32.store, memory.size, and memory.grow. NO loads, NO Data segments, NO i64/narrow stores, NO globals/calls/tables/imports, NO validation execution, NO floats (those stay deferred). Additive to M0/M1/M2: manifest_m0.json and manifest_m2.json are NOT modified by this milestone.

spec.role: SEMANTIC ORACLE (authoritative). SAME frozen pin as M0/M1/M2; we author no expected outputs. The `expected` values (and assert_trap `text`) in the converted JSON were authored by the reference interpreter.

wabt.role: TOOLCHAIN ONLY (wast2json / wasm2wat / wasm-objdump). NOT the semantic oracle.

conversion.features_note: IDENTICAL guardrail to manifest_m0.json / manifest_m2.json (same converted data discipline). Under --disable-bulk-memory, memory.wast / data.wast / align.wast REJECT at conversion at pin 82cd4f9 (bulk-memory / passive-data-segment syntax) -- that is why those obvious 'memory' files are NOT M3 targets. f32/f64 are MVP-core and cannot be disabled by any flag, so integer-value purity is enforced by MANIFEST CURATION (see targets/excluded), not by conversion flags.

targets_note: Integer-value-clean, linear-memory-bearing files whose instantiated modules use ONLY sections {Type,Function,Export,Code,Memory}, opcodes in the M1 integer set + the M2 control-flow set + {i32.store, memory.size, memory.grow}, and memory limits in {min-only, min+max}; no load/Data/i64-store/narrow-store/global/table/call/float. Selected FROM DATA (a read-only probe converting every memory-bearing candidate under the guardrail flags above and measuring module float purity + sections/opcodes/limits via wasm-objdump; a confirming sweep over all 97 test/core files found NO other integer-pure MVP-memory file). Evidence: goal-runs/m3-linear-memory/scope.txt. Coverage: store.wast -> i32.store executing inside block/loop/if/else/br/br_if/br_table/return (memory declaration + in-bounds store, no trap); memory_size.wast -> memory.size / memory.grow page-and-limit arithmetic across invokes, INCLUDING grow-past-declared-max failure (the (memory 0 2) module's grow 3 / grow 4 are REFUSED; because the exported grow drops memory.grow's -1 result, the oracle observes the refusal only via the FOLLOWING memory.size asserts showing the page count unchanged -- the -1 return value itself is unit-tested, not oracle-checked). Neither target contains a Data section, an assert_trap, or a load opcode -- so loads, data-segment init, and out-of-bounds traps are DEFERRED (the OOB trap path is implemented for i32.store correctness and unit-tested, its trap string read from the oracle JSON of memory_grow/memory_trap, but no in-scope target exercises it).

excluded.reason: REJECT AT CONVERSION under the guardrail flags (--disable-bulk-memory): at pin 82cd4f9 these use bulk-memory / passive-data-segment syntax. Genuinely out of MVP-linear-memory scope. Excluded explicitly (not silently skipped); revisit at a bulk-memory milestone.

HAS-FLOAT: the instantiated modules mix f32/f64 load/store into the same bodies (and carry f32/f64 assert operands), failing the body/operand integer-purity discipline. memory_trap.wast is where out-of-bounds traps live, but it is float-contaminated. Outside M3 integer-value scope. Excluded explicitly; revisit with floats (M5).

Need Global/Table/Elem(/Import) sections plus call / call_indirect / select -- their instantiated modules test load/grow embedded in call/table/global machinery. Outside M3's memory-only scope. Excluded explicitly; the i32.store OPCODE is still exercised (by store.wast) and memory.size/grow by memory_size.wast. Revisit with the calls/globals/tables milestones. (Canonical trap text 'out of bounds memory access' is read from these files' converted JSON for the i32.store bounds check, without adopting the files as targets.)

Integer-pure and sections in-scope, but declares (memory 0) with ZERO memory opcodes and ZERO value assertions (1 module command only) -- it tests inline-module SYNTAX, not linear-memory SEMANTICS, and its (memory 0) shape is already covered by memory_size.wast. Excluded to keep M3 targets semantically memory-bearing.
